author: barnabasbusa
content: How about exclude all attester duties? 😄

author: barnabasbusa
content: I know that would be a significantly more work

author: barnabasbusa
content: but I think with mass slashings we could have some issues with not having enough ptc votes

author: barnabasbusa
content: maybe thats for a new EIP tho

author: potuz
content: no, I am against that one

author: potuz
content: not only is more work but also adds a lot of bug surface on the states we use for validation

author: potuz
content: if we have mass slashings we are screwed anyway, this should be part of the consensus mentality

author: potuz
content: we **will need** social coordination in that case anyway

author: nflaig
content: I don't think it is that much more work? you just remove them from shuffling?

author: barnabasbusa
content: once you open that box, there is a bunch of other stuff in there

author: potuz
content: but it forces you to have the right state on validation

author: potuz
content: today we use the head state as long as we're finalizing

author: barnabasbusa
content: I think we should keep scope of 8045 limited to proposals only

author: potuz
content: now you'll be screwed cause your committee will be wrong if you' re validating an attestation on a fork with a slashed validator

author: nflaig
content: don't you just look at the state of the dependent root?

author: barnabasbusa
content: and if we wanna do an 'all validator duties' exclusion, we should have another eip for it

author: potuz
content: for sure, the attestation thing is going in over my dead body

author: potuz
content: dependent root is not good enough, some slashings are processed immediately, 8045 as speced today only checks in the lookahead which is fine

author: potuz
content: if you do this for attesters you need to make sure that the committees are set 2 epochs in advance and they stay like that regardless of slashings

author: potuz
content: if that's specified this way I don't mind

author: nflaig
content: what do  you actually gain from excluding them from attesters, smaller committees? because aren't we ignoring their weight anyways when counting attestations?

author: barnabasbusa
content: we would still expect attestation duties from a slashed validator

author: barnabasbusa
content: but the slashed validator won't be able to do any validator duties

author: nflaig
content: yes but for attestations they don't take away a spot of a unslashed validator

author: barnabasbusa
content: do you filter for slashed validators when it comes to attestations and duties?

author: barnabasbusa
content: I thought currently slashed validators are still counted as a normal validator, and we the whole network just take it as a penality of missing duties

author: jtraglia
content: <@412614104222531604> can you update the consensus-specs PR? It's still says "from all duties" everywhere.

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah will do in a sec

author: barnabasbusa
content: what about sync committees/ptc?

author: barnabasbusa
content: PTAL

author: nflaig
content: so I still don't see why that matters, since for attesters, they don't take away a spot of an unslashed attester, what you mean is we should not count their balance in `get_total_active_balance`?

author: nflaig
content: but why not ptc?

author: jtraglia
content: Much better. Thank you 🙂

author: potuz
content: So PTC is not **that** bad. The point is that you may incorrectly count a PTC attestation over gossip because you used a wrong state to validate it. But a consensus bug is less likely to happen cause on block processing we always use the parent state that has the right committee, but we may still see a forkchoice split.

author: barnabasbusa
content: what would happen if the majority of the ptc are slashed validators?

author: potuz
content: Slashed an malicious?

author: potuz
content: they could vote agains the payload and then the next builders may be able to reorg that payload, in the worst case scenario all blocks are empty

author: potuz
content: this grieves builders

author: barnabasbusa
content: I don't understand? slashed validators won't be able to complete their duties no?

author: nflaig
content: no, I think ptc votes are considered exactly the same and selection doesn't filter either right now

author: barnabasbusa
content: If I have a slashed validator, will I be able to still vote correct on the PTC vote if my validator comes up for duties?

author: barnabasbusa
content: or will that vote be considered invalid, as it came from a slashed validator?

author: barnabasbusa
content: not sure how the weight of the ptc committee members calculated

author: nflaig
content: no it's considered valid, I believe the sync committee works the same way

author: barnabasbusa
content: Right, so we could in theory end up in a situation where we have a bunch of slashed validators and PTC won't be able to reach majority

author: barnabasbusa
content: Question is, do we care? 😄

author: potuz
content: they will reach majority

author: potuz
content: if the slashed validators are still being run

author: potuz
content: if they are honest (slashed by a bug) then no harm

author: nflaig
content: we have the ptc window though, how can you load the wrong state? or you mean you cannot load a viable state so you have to ignore the message?

author: potuz
content: if they are dishonest they could cause harm

author: potuz
content: ahh stupid me, you mean not worry about those recently slashed, but accept anything in the window? yes, that works fine by me

author: potuz
content: so as long as the committee is set in the state I'm fine adding that

author: nflaig
content: yes same as for proposers, we have same lookahead for ptc

author: potuz
content: yeah I do not oppose adding those, those are simple both in implementation and in bug surface

author: potuz
content: anything which the committee is not fixed on any state descending from the same dependent root is a problem

author: nflaig
content: ok I have ptc also implemented already, ready to push 😄

content: but also, this requires a bit more work, since you need to go through the indices, so we should see if this is even meaningful
